<title level="1" right="Relic Major Gift">[Form of Fury](/Relics.aspx?ID=10)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Gamemastery Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=22) pg. 97

<row gap="medium">
**Aspect** Beast

**Prerequisite** The relic is a worn item.
</row>

</column>

---

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, envision;  **Frequency** twice per day;   **Effect** Calling upon the bestial nature within, you change shape into that of an animal. The relic casts [_animal form_](/Spells.aspx?ID=10) on you, transforming you into the same type of animal every time, chosen when the gift is gained; the spell level is half the item’s level, rounded down.

 At 11th level, the spell is 5th level, and the spell level goes up by 1 for every 2 levels beyond that.
name: Form of Fury
prerequisite: The relic is a worn item.

<title level="1" right="Relic Major Gift">[Steal Luck](/Relics.aspx?ID=101)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Contingency" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=385" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 199

<row gap="medium">
**Aspect** Luck

</row>

</column>

---

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day; ; **Trigger** You or a creature within 30 feet of you rolls two d20s for a [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=76) or [misfortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=110) effect and you like the higher roll;  **Effect** You steal the higher of the two numbers the triggering target rolled; the target must use the other result. Until you make your next daily preparations, you can substitute that number for one d20 roll made for a saving throw or skill check you have to make against an effect created by an enemy, hazard, or the environment. Doing so is a fortune effect. If you fail to do so before your next daily preparations, you lose the stored number.
name: Steal Luck

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 200 **Aspect** Time **Prerequisite** The relic is 5th level or higher. --- **Activate** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Effect** You target one creature within 30 feet, temporarily imposing the decrepitude of time on it. The target must attempt a Fortitude saving throw. The [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3) and [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13) conditions last 1 minute. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target becomes clumsy 1 and enfeebled 1.
**Failure** The target becomes clumsy 2, enfeebled 2, and [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10).
**Critical Failure** The target becomes clumsy 3, enfeebled 3, and drained 2.
name: Sands of the Hourglass
prerequisite: The relic is 5th level or higher.

<title level="1" right="Relic Minor Gift">[Intelligent Relic](/Relics.aspx?ID=109)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Intelligent" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=280" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 200

<row gap="medium">
**Aspect** Intelligent Relic

</row>

</column>

---

Your relic becomes an intelligent item. The relic possesses precise sight at a range of 30 feet and imprecise hearing at a range of 30 feet. It is trained in [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=2298), increasing its proficiency when you increase yours. Choose whether it can speak aloud or communicate with you telepathically. It knows languages fitting its backstory, usually from among those you know.

The relic is also trained in three skills, increasing one skill’s proficiency each time the relic gains a new gift. Your relic’s mental attribute modifiers begin at +3, +2, and +1, arranged as you choose. The relic is also trained in Will saves, and its proficiency increases as yours does. Each time you gain a new gift, increase one of the relic’s mental attribute modifiers by 1. The relic otherwise functions as normal for an intelligent item.

If you’re [sanctified](/Traits.aspx?ID=519) to [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) or to [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521), you can choose to have your relic sanctified in the same way.
name: Intelligent Relic

<title level="1" right="Relic Minor Gift">[Creative Spark](/Relics.aspx?ID=110)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Fire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=72" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Highhelm](/Sources.aspx?ID=204) pg. 114

<row gap="medium">
**Aspect** Forge

</row>

</column>

---

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You strike the relic against a piece of flint and summon a [fire wisp](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1144) to assist you, which appears in an unoccupied square next to you. The fire wisp doesn't act in combat, but it can assist you while you [Craft](/Actions.aspx?ID=43) items. You gain a +1 bonus to [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) checks involving blacksmithing and general metalworking, increasing to +2 at 9th level and +3 at 17th level. Once per day, when you roll a critical failure on a Crafting check related to blacksmithing, you get a failure instead. The wisp replaces the need for a forge while blacksmithing, but you still require other equipment and tools. The wisp remains for up to 10 minutes or until you Dismiss it. You can extend this duration by an additional 10 minutes if you feed the wisp one light Bulk worth of charcoal or firewood, or one pint of oil.
name: Creative Spark

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You strike the relic against a non-magical metal object of 4 Bulk or less, repairing it. The object's edges glow white hot, and you restore 5 Hit Points to the object per level of the relic. markdown:
<title level="1" right="Relic Major Gift">[Elements of Creation](/Relics.aspx?ID=113)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Air" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=5" />
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Earth" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=55" />
<trait label="Water" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=165" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Highhelm](/Sources.aspx?ID=204) pg. 114

<row gap="medium">
**Aspect** Forge

**Prerequisite** creative spark gift
</row>

</column>

---

When you activate creative spark, you can choose to summon an [air wisp](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1142), a [water wisp](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1145), and an [earth wisp](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1143) in unoccupied squares next to you, in addition to the fire wisp. Unlike a typical earth wisp, the summoned earth wisp takes the form of a small iron creature resembling an anvil crossed with a horned beetle. Like the fire wisp, these additional wisps don't act in combat but can assist you while you [Craft](/Actions.aspx?ID=43) items. While all four wisps are assisting you, the bonus you gain from creative spark is doubled, and your outcome for [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) checks related to blacksmithing is one degree of success better than your roll. In addition, being assisted by all four wisps replaces the need for any equipment other than blacksmithing tools when crafting metal items. The wisps last for up to 10 minutes or until you Dismiss them all together as an action. If you extend the duration of the fire wisp, it also extends the duration for the other wisps.
name: Elements of Creation

text: Elements of Creation Source Highhelm pg. 114 Aspect Forge Prerequisite creative spark gift --- When you activate creative spark, you can choose to summon an air wisp, a water wisp, and an earth wisp in unoccupied squares next to you, in addition to the fire wisp. Unlike a typical earth wisp, the summoned earth wisp takes the form of a small iron creature resembling an anvil crossed with a horned beetle. Like the fire wisp, these additional wisps don't act in combat but can assist you while you Craft items. While all four wisps are assisting you, the bonus you gain from creative spark is doubled, and your outcome for Crafting checks related to blacksmithing is one degree of success better than your roll. In addition, being assisted by all four wisps replaces the need for any equipment other than blacksmithing tools when crafting metal items. The wisps last for up to 10 minutes or until you Dismiss them all together as an action. If you extend the duration of the fire wisp, it also extends the duration for the other wisps.

<title level="1" right="Relic Major Gift">[Molten Crucible](/Relics.aspx?ID=114)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Fire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=72" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Highhelm](/Sources.aspx?ID=204) pg. 114

<row gap="medium">
**Aspect** Forge

</row>

</column>

---

**Activate** <actions string="Three Actions" /> command, Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You strike your relic against a weapon, armor, or shield made of metal. The item becomes red hot and, over the next 10 minutes, transforms into a different base item from the same category. This changes the item's base form while retaining its same materials, runes, and magical properties. Both base items must have an item level of 1 or less, and weapons must require the same number of hands. Specific items can't be changed in this way unless the GM expressly allows it. For example, a _+2 [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375) [mace](/Weapons.aspx?ID=7)_ can become a _+2 cold iron [longsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=31)_, or [chain mail](/Armor.aspx?ID=9) can become [half plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=12), but a [moonlit chain](/Equipment.aspx?ID=156) can't be affected in this way.
name: Molten Crucible

**Source** [Highhelm](/Sources.aspx?ID=204) pg. 114 **Aspect** Forge --- **Activate** command, Interact; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The relic is absorbed into your body, and you transform into an elemental being of red-hot steel. You gain the effects of 7th-level [_elemental form_](/Spells.aspx?ID=98) and use the statistics for the forge battle form below. In this form, you can re-shape your limbs into different objects, such as a blade or hammer, by using an Interact action. Changing your limbs in this way allows you to choose between bludgeoning, physical, or slashing damage.
  • **Forge Speed** 30 feet; fire resistance 10, weakness 5 to cold and water; **Melee** forgestrike, **Damage** 1d10 modular; 1d4 [persistent fire](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29).
name: Living Forge

markdown:
**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 148 **Aspect** Metal --- Your relic is engraved with nodes and elements only visible from up close, lacking only a source of current for its machinations. You gain resistance to electricity equal to half the level of your relic (minimum 1). **Activate—Complete the Circuit** ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Trigger** You are targeted by or are in the area of an [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=56) effect; **Effect** Your relic uses the triggering effect to complete its circuit. You choose which path the electricity takes down the circuit, gaining the corresponding benefit.
  • **Capacitor** You become electrically charged, gaining a +1 status bonus to attack and damage rolls until the end of your next turn.
  • **Inductor** Your resistance against electricity damage increases to the level of your relic until the end of your next turn.
  • **Resistor** Your relic attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) the effect. On a failure, your relic overheats, dealing fire damage to you equal to the counteract rank of the effect (or double the counteract rank on a critical failure).
name: Enhancing Circuitry

<title level="1" right="Relic Minor Gift">[Scrap Shot](/Relics.aspx?ID=117)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Metal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=505" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 148

<row gap="medium">
**Aspect** Metal

</row>

</column>

---

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))    **Effect** Magnetic repulsion sends a sharp chunk of metal scrap flying from your relic. Make a spell attack roll with your relic against a target within 30 feet. The chunk deals 1d6 piercing damage for every 2 levels the relic has (minimum 1d6). If the target is wearing metal armor or is made of metal, you gain a +1 circumstance bonus to your spell attack roll, and the target becomes [clumsy 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3) until the start of your next turn as the scrap adheres to the target. If your relic is made of a [precious](/Traits.aspx?ID=131) metal, the shot is also made of or coated in that metal, bypassing resistances and the like accordingly. The scrap left behind by the shot rusts or crumbles quickly and isn't worth anything if sold.
name: Scrap Shot

<traits>
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

Whenever you use the relic’s form of fury activation, it can cast [_aerial form_](/Spells.aspx?ID=4) or [_dinosaur form_](/Spells.aspx?ID=72) instead of [_animal form_](/Spells.aspx?ID=10). Like form of fury, these spells also turn you into the same type of animal each time (so you have three forms you can assume in total, one from each spell). The spell level is the same as defined in form of fury.

 While transformed, you can switch from one of the three forms to another by Sustaining the Activation. This doesn’t change the duration.

<title level="1" right="Relic Grand Gift">[All Will Decay](/Relics.aspx?ID=121)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Metal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=505" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 148

<row gap="medium">
**Aspect** Metal

</row>

</column>

---

Your relic's link to the [Plane of Metal](/Planes.aspx?ID=24) tunes it in to the inevitability of rust and decay. You can cast 5th-rank [_rust cloud_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1377) at will as an innate spell.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** Rusted flakes swirl in a cloud around you, creating a 15-foot emanation that lasts for 1 minute. You're [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) to anyone outside the cloud. An enemy that starts its turn in the cloud takes 15 slashing damage with a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save. If the target has the [metal](/Traits.aspx?ID=505) trait or is primarily made of metal, the damage is 30 instead. Unattended non-magical metal items within the cloud automatically become broken.
name: All Will Decay

You can Sustain this activation up to 1 minute.

<title level="1" right="Relic Grand Gift">[Form of Vengeance](/Relics.aspx?ID=135)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 310

<row gap="medium">
**Aspect** Beast

**Prerequisite** form of fury gift
</row>

</column>

---

Whenever you use the relic's form of fury activation, it can cast [_aerial form_](/Spells.aspx?ID=4) or [_dinosaur form_](/Spells.aspx?ID=72) instead of [_animal form_](/Spells.aspx?ID=10). Like form of fury, these spells also turn you into the same type of animal each time (so you have three forms you can assume in total, one from each spell). The spell rank is the same as defined in form of fury.

 While transformed, you can switch from one of the three forms to another by Sustaining the activation. This doesn't change the duration.
name: Form of Vengeance

<title level="1" right="Relic Minor Gift">[Living Death](/Relics.aspx?ID=143)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 312

<row gap="medium">
**Aspect** Death

</row>

</column>

---

You stand on the threshold between the living and the dead. If you attempt a saving throw against an effect that would deal void damage to you, such as [_harm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=146), your outcome is one degree of success better than what you rolled. If you roll a critical success and the effect is capable of healing [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160), you regain HP equal to half the spell's full damage. Any [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509) spell or effect that would heal you has only half the normal effect. All these effects apply only if you are a living creature.
name: Living Death

<title level="1" right="Relic Major Gift">[Pierce the Veil](/Relics.aspx?ID=145)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 312

<row gap="medium">
**Aspect** Death

**Prerequisite** living death gift
</row>

</column>

---

The relic allows you to pierce the veil between life and death. While you are holding or wearing the relic, you have the [void healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42) ability (as though you were [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160); vitality energy harms you and void energy heals you). In addition, whenever you are healed by a [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) spell or effect, you gain resistance 5 to all damage (except [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=75), _[ghost touch](/Equipment.aspx?ID=297)_, [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=149), and [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509)) until the start of your next turn. Finally, you gain a +2 item bonus to all saves against [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects while you are the bearer of the relic; this increases to a +3 item bonus at 13th level.
name: Pierce the Veil

<title level="1" right="Relic Grand Gift">[Grim Specter](/Relics.aspx?ID=147)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Polymorph" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=670" />
<trait label="Void" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=510" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 312

<row gap="medium">
**Aspect** Death

</row>

</column>

---

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127), [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You can call upon the terrifying powers that fuel your relic to transform yourself into a specter of death. You become [incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=222), having no physical body. You are immune to effects that require a physical body, including precision damage as well as exposure to most [diseases](/Traits.aspx?ID=578) and [poisons](/Traits.aspx?ID=669). You also have resistance to all damage equal to half the relic's level; [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=75) damage, _[ghost touch](/Equipment.aspx?ID=297)_ attacks, [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=149) damage, [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) damage, and [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509) damage ignore this resistance, and the resistance doubles against non-magical damage.

 You can move through solid objects. When inside an object, you can't perceive, attack, or Interact with anything outside it, and you're always [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) on turns you start in an object. Corporeal creatures can pass through you but can't end their movement in your space. You can't attempt Strength-based checks against corporeal creatures or objects, unless those objects have the _ghost touch_ property rune. Likewise, corporeal creatures can't attempt Strength-based checks against you. 

You can Sustain the activation up to 1 minute.
name: Grim Specter

You become incorporeal, having no physical body. You are immune to effects that require a physical body, including precision damage as well as exposure to most diseases and poisons. You also have resistance to all damage equal to half the relic's level; force damage, ghost touch attacks, spirit damage, void damage, and vitality damage ignore this resistance, and the resistance doubles against non-magical damage. You can move through solid objects. When inside an object, you can't perceive, attack, or Interact with anything outside it, and you're always slowed 1 on turns you start in an object. Corporeal creatures can pass through you but can't end their movement in your space. Likewise, corporeal creatures can't attempt Strength-based checks against you. You can Sustain the activation up to 1 minute.

Your relic draws strength from the earth underfoot to lighten your load. As long as your relic is on your person and you are standing on the ground, you can carry 1 more Bulk than normal before becoming [encumbered](/Conditions.aspx?ID=12), and you increase your maximum Bulk by 2. At 6th level and every 4 levels thereafter, increase the Bulk you can carry before becoming encumbered by 1 and your maximum Bulk by 2.
name: Ease Burden

<title level="1" right="Relic Minor Gift">[Rolling Geode](/Relics.aspx?ID=149)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Earth" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=585" />
</traits>

---

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** Stone and crystal form a sphere that chases your foes. You create a geode in an unoccupied square within 30 feet. You can spend a single action, which has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) trait, to move the geode up to 30 feet along the ground. If the geode enters a creature's space, the creature must succeed at a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save or take 2d6 bludgeoning damage; a creature can take damage this way only once per turn, even if you roll the geode through its space multiple times.  The geode persists for up to 1 minute or until destroyed or Dismissed; it has AC 5, Hardness 10, and 40 Hit Points, and is immune to critical hits and precision damage.
name: Rolling Geode

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Relic Major Gift">[Underground Bounty](/Relics.aspx?ID=152)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Earth" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=585" />
<trait label="Metal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=505" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 313

<row gap="medium">
**Aspect** Earth

</row>

</column>

---

Your relic produces metals and stones to empower your weapons. Each day during your daily preparations, your relic conjures a magical shard of [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=277), [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=272), gold, or a non-precious stone or metal material of your choice (such as bronze or granite). If your relic is a weapon, it immediately absorbs the shard, causing it to gain a magical sheen of that metal or stone until your next daily preparations. If your relic is not a weapon, the shard is not immediately used, and you can touch it to a stone or metal weapon using an Interact action to give it a magical sheen of that metal or stone for 10 minutes. While coated in the magical sheen, the weapon interacts with weaknesses, resistances, and the like as if it were made of the stone or metal from the shard rather than its own. The sheen and the shard are obviously magical in nature, and if you don't use the shard, it disappears when you make your next daily preparations. At 17th level, add [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=271) and [dawnsilver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=275) to the available options.
name: Underground Bounty

---

The fiendish power of your relic offers you more protection against celestials and allows you to ignore harm. While wearing the relic, you gain a +1 status bonus to AC against attacks by [celestials](/Languages.aspx?ID=16) and resistance to an energy type corresponding to the relic's fiendish origin (such as fire resistance from a [devil](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=30)). The resistance is equal to half the relic's level (minimum 1).

<title level="1" right="Relic Minor Gift">[Little Helper](/Relics.aspx?ID=156)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Unholy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=521" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 313

<row gap="medium">
**Aspect** Fiend

</row>

</column>

---

Your relic awakens to sapience, developing a personality (usually one that clashes with your own). Your relic has a personality corresponding to the type of [fiend](/Traits.aspx?ID=70) associated with the item, the [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521) trait, [precise](/Rules.aspx?ID=411) vision and [imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412) hearing each out to 30 feet, the ability to understand and speak one language you know, and Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma modifiers of +0. Your item's total Perception and Will modifiers are equal to your proficiency modifiers in Perception and Will, respectively. It's trained in the [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) associated with its fiend type (such as Demon Lore), for a total modifier of 2 + the relic's level. Unlike most intelligent items, the relic has no actions or reactions and can't use any of its own activations.
name: Little Helper

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 313 **Aspect** Fiend --- **Activate** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You bargain with the evil force empowering your relic for a boon, though you must trade something in return. Select one of the following benefits to gain.
  • Your relic attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) a harmful condition affecting you.
  • You regain 3d8+16 Hit Points. This is a [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=89) effect.
  • You gain a +2 status bonus to attack rolls for 1 minute.
One of the following randomly determined effects occurs to you in payment, after applying the benefit.
  • You are [stupefied 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) for 1 minute.
  • You are [clumsy 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) for 1 minute.
  • You lose 1d8 Hit Points.
  • You take a –1 penalty to damage rolls for 1 minute.
name: Fiendish Bargain

<traits>

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))    **Effect** Profane energies twist your weapons, filling them with rage toward everything good. For 1 minute, your weapons and [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199) attacks gain the [_unholy_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=307) weapon rune while you hold them. If a weapon is at its limit on property runes, you can choose one property rune on the weapon to go dormant while the _unholy_ rune takes effect. In addition, Strikes your allies make while they're within 30 feet of you gain the [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521) trait.
name: Profane Fervor

<title level="1" right="Relic Grand Gift">[Command Legion](/Relics.aspx?ID=159)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Unholy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=521" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 314

<row gap="medium">
**Aspect** Fiend

</row>

</column>

---

**Activate** <actions string="Three Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32))    **Effect** Your relic opens a gate within itself, overwhelming your enemies with fiends. You cast 6th-rank [summon fiend](/Traits.aspx?ID=600), but summon two fiends instead of one, and you can command them both with 1 action to Sustain a spell. If the relic has the profane fervor gift, this activation grants the effects of that gift as well, without expending its daily use. For the next minute, you can use the following activation.

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32))    **Effect** You summon that same fiend again in an adjacent space. The fiend returns at full Hit Points and all conditions, spells, and other effects on it end before you summon it again. However, it does not recover any limited-use abilities it used before, such as innate spells or abilities with a Frequency entry.
name: Command Legion

<title level="1" right="Relic Minor Gift">[Heat Haze](/Relics.aspx?ID=161)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Fire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=604" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 314

<row gap="medium">
**Aspect** Fire

</row>

</column>

---

Your relic's heat keeps the air around you pleasantly warm. While you are holding or wearing the relic, it protects you from severe environmental cold. At 9th level, it also protects you from extreme cold, and at 17th level, it protects you from incredible cold. At 4th level, the relic also gains the following activation.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** Your relic erratically elevates the air temperature around you, creating heat shimmers that distort your appearance and grant you the [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) condition for 1 minute. As the nature of this effect still leaves your location obvious, you can't use this concealment to [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=62) or [Sneak](/Actions.aspx?ID=63).
name: Heat Haze

<trait label="Fire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=604" />

---

Fire magic suffuses your body and soul, protecting you from lesser flames and allowing you to kindle even the smallest sparks into powerful infernos. You gain fire resistance 10. The first time each hour you are targeted by a fire effect that would deal damage, you regain 1d8 HP for every [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) rank of the effect, in addition to taking the damage.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32))  **Trigger** A creature within 60 feet is critically hit by or critically fails a save against a [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) effect and is not reduced to 0 Hit Points;  **Effect** Flames roar forth from the triggering creature, dealing 6d6 fire damage to all creatures in a 15-foot burst centered on it, including that creature ([basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex). You aren't affected by the activation, though your allies are. If this damage reduces the triggering creature to 0 Hit Points, it's reduced to a fine ash, though its gear remains. You can't use this activation again for 1d4 rounds.
name: Blazing Soul

<title level="1" right="Relic Major Gift">[Righteous Call](/Relics.aspx?ID=17)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Good" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=83" />
</traits>

<row gap="medium">
**Aspect** Celestial

</row>

</column>

---

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** Holy magic fills your armaments, making them true weapons against evil. For 1 minute, your weapons and unarmed attacks gain the [_holy_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=299) weapon rune while you hold them. If a weapon is at its limit on property runes, you can choose one property rune on the weapon to go dormant while the _holy_ rune takes effect. In addition, any successful Strikes your allies make while they’re within 30 feet of you deal 1 additional good damage.
name: Righteous Call

<traits>
<trait label="Healing" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=623" />

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32))  **Trigger** An ally within 60 feet regains more Hit Points than it is currently down via a targeted [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=89) [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509) effect without a duration;  **Effect** Your relic acts as a relay for vitality. You or an adjacent willing creature regain a number of Hit Points equal to the amount in excess of the triggering creature's maximum Hit Points; for instance, if the triggering creature was missing 5 Hit Points and was targeted by a [_heal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=148) spell that restored 12 Hit Points, you or an adjacent willing creature would regain the remaining 7 Hit Points.
name: Vitality Siphon

<traits>
<trait label="Healing" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=623" />

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** Massive amounts of vitality energy surge forth. For 1 minute, you emit vitality energy in a 10-foot emanation centered on you. At the end of your turn, you (if you are living) and allied living creatures in the emanation regain 10 Hit Points. If you or an allied creature would die due to an increased [dying](/Conditions.aspx?ID=11) value, you can Dismiss the activation as a free action to prevent the death; if you do, their dying value doesn't increase.
name: Life Everlasting

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 316 **Aspect** Mind --- **Activate** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** Your relic reaches into the minds of nearby creatures and blocks your presence from their perceptions. For the next 10 minutes, a creature that enters a 120-foot-radius emanation, centered on you, must attempt a secret Will save, after which the creature is then temporarily immune for 1 day. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes a –2 status penalty to Perception checks to [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=84) or otherwise detect you.
**Failure** You filter all of the creature's senses, making it difficult for it to notice you. When you are [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=22) or [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=39) from the creature, it doesn't observe you as soon as you do anything other than [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=62), [Sneak](/Actions.aspx?ID=63), or Step. If it rolls a Perception check to Seek or otherwise detect you, it gets the outcome one degree of success worse than what it rolled, and if you roll a [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) check to Hide, Sneak, or otherwise escape its attention, you get the outcome one degree of success better than what you rolled against the creature. If you use a [hostile action](/Rules.aspx?ID=300) toward the creature, the effect ends for that creature after your hostile action is completed.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the effect doesn't end for that creature if you use a hostile action against it.
name: Perception Filter

Your relic grows potent natural medicines. Each night, two medicinal herbs (or other plants) grow from your relic and can be harmlessly harvested during your next daily preparations. A living creature that consumes an herb with an Interact action regains 1d8 Hit Points for every 2 relic levels (minimum 1d8). At 9th level, the relic grows three herbs each night, and at 17th level, the relic grows four herbs each night. Herbs wither away 1 day after being harvested.

text: Sprout Ally Source GM Core pg. 317 Aspect Plant --- Activate Two Actions (concentrate, manipulate) Frequency once per day; Effect Your relic rapidly matures and animates nearby plant matter into an ally to fight by you, casting summon plant or fungus , with a rank of half the item's level rounded up. You can Sustain this activation as you would the spell.

<title level="1" right="Relic Major Gift">[Grappling Vine](/Relics.aspx?ID=181)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Plant" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=668" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 317

<row gap="medium">
**Aspect** Plant

</row>

</column>

---

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=15), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))    **Effect** Your relic launches a sticky, multi-stranded vine at a target within 50 feet. Make an attack roll as you would when using a [grappling hook](/Equipment.aspx?ID=23), but if you roll a critical failure on the check to secure the vine, you get a failure instead. Once the vine is anchored, creatures receive a +1 status bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) checks to [Climb](/Actions.aspx?ID=33) the vine, and they gain a +5-foot status bonus to the distance they move with a successful check while Climbing using the vine. The vine lasts for 1 day or until you use grappling vine again, at which point your older vine withers.
name: Grappling Vine

<title level="1" right="Relic Major Gift">[Petal Dance](/Relics.aspx?ID=182)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Plant" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=668" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 317

<row gap="medium">
**Aspect** Plant

</row>

</column>

---

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** You discorporate into a cloud of petals and leaves. This grants you the [swarm](/Traits.aspx?ID=239) trait, immunity to [falling](/Rules.aspx?ID=407) damage, resistance 5 to bludgeoning and slashing damage, and weakness 5 to area and [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=150) damage. You can fit into spaces only a few inches wide, moving your constituent petals through the gap. You don't gain the [swarm mind](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=36) ability, so you're still affected normally by [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106) effects. As a swarm, you can't speak, cast spells, use [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104) actions requiring your hands, activate your magic items, or make any of your Strikes with your normal body. You remain in this form for 1 minute or until you Dismiss the activation. At 13th level, the relic gains a reaction that triggers when you fall or take damage, applying this gift's resistances, weaknesses, and immunity to falling damage to the triggering effect.
name: Petal Dance

markdown:
**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 317 **Aspect** Plant --- **Activate** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** With a pulse of natural energy, your relic grows massive plants in an instant. The megaflora is of one of the following types, chosen by the GM when the relic gains this gift. A megaflora appears in an unoccupied 10-foot space within 30 feet and has 50 Hit Points, weakness 5 to fire, AC 37, Reflex +20, and Fortitude +30. It persists for 1 minute or until reduced to 0 Hit Points.
  • **Corpseflower** A single putrid-smelling flower grows at the target location. While the flower persists, each round at the end of your turn, all creatures in a 20-foot burst centered on the plant except you must succeed at a Fortitude save or be [sickened 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) (also [stunned 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93) on a critical failure). This is a [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126) effect.
  • **Thorns** A 10-foot-tall thorned plant or bamboo stalk grows at the target location. While the stalk persists, each round at the end of your turn, sharp stakes erupt from the ground, dealing 6d8 piercing damage to all creatures in a 20-foot burst centered on the plant except you ([basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex).
  • **Tree of Life** A large tree bearing life-giving fruit grows at the target location. While the tree persists, living creatures within reach of the tree can use an Interact action to pick and eat one of the tree's fruits, regaining 2d8+5 Hit Points; picking a fruit without eating it causes the fruit to instantly vanish. This is a [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=89) and [wood](/Traits.aspx?ID=508) effect, and a given creature can heal from the tree only once per round.
name: Megaflora

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Relic Minor Gift">[Encompassing Darkness](/Relics.aspx?ID=184)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Shadow" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=693" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 317

<row gap="medium">
**Aspect** Shadow

</row>

</column>

---

The shadows at your feet can hold objects. You can Interact with your shadow to store or remove objects, just like you would a mundane container. Your shadow can contain 3 Bulk of objects, which don't count toward the Bulk you are carrying. At 6th level, and every 4 levels thereafter, you can store 1 additional Bulk of objects in your shadow. While the items are in your shadow and can be detected normally, you gain a +4 circumstance bonus to [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) checks to [Conceal the Objects](/Actions.aspx?ID=61) unless someone knows to check your shadow for items.
name: Encompassing Darkness

Your relic absorbs light and wraps shadow around you, hiding you in darkness. **Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))    **Effect** Your relic pulls at nearby shadows, twisting them into the shape of a simple weapon or a simple tool or item, such as a [rope](/Equipment.aspx?ID=44) or [crowbar](/Equipment.aspx?ID=18). It lasts until it's used for a single activity, until you use shadow smith again, or for 1 minute, whichever comes first, after which it dissipates.

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Relic Major Gift">[Dancing Shadow](/Relics.aspx?ID=187)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Shadow" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=693" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 318

<row gap="medium">
**Aspect** Shadow

**Prerequisite** The relic is a weapon.
</row>

</column>

---

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** Your relic's shadow detaches from your relic and dances through the air to attack an enemy. When you Activate the relic, designate a target. The relic's shadow flies up to 60 feet until it's adjacent to that foe and then makes a Strike against it, dealing 5d8 damage on a success or double that on a critical success; the damage type is of any type normally dealt by your relic. The shadow uses your attack bonus with the relic, and it uses and contributes to your multiple attack penalty. While this activation is in effect, you can use a single action, which has the [attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=15) and [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) traits, to mentally direct the shadow to make another Strike against the same target. The shadow lasts until that target is reduced to 0 Hit Points, that target moves more than 400 feet from you, or that target moves to an area where no shadow could be cast, such as an area of complete [darkness](/Rules.aspx?ID=409), whichever comes first.

 The shadow doesn't take up space, grant flanking, or have any other attributes a creature would, and it automatically follows the chosen foe. The shadow can't make any attack other than its Strike, and feats or spells that affect weapons do not apply to it.
name: Dancing Shadow

<traits>
<trait label="Shadow" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=693" />
<trait label="Teleportation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=710" />

---

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32))    **Effect** Your relic creates a path from your shadow to a nearby one, teleporting you and any items you're wearing or holding from your current space to an unoccupied one within 30 feet that you can see. You can't use the dark roads gift again for 1d4 rounds.

 If the destination is not within an area of [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=407), the range is instead 60 feet. If this would bring another creature with you—even if you're carrying it in an [extradimensional](/Traits.aspx?ID=67) container—the gift fails.

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 318 **Aspect** Water --- **Activate** ([attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=540), [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You call down a curtain of violent rain in a location within 120 feet. The wall is 5 feet thick, 30 feet long, up to 30 feet high, and lasts for 1 minute. The wall stands vertically, but you can shape its path. The wall has the following effects.
  • If a [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) effect crosses through the wall, it either uses the outcome one degree of success worse than the result of its attack roll or its targets use the outcome one degree of success better than the result of their saving throw, as appropriate.
  • Creatures with a weakness to [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=165) that cross the wall or start their turn in the wall take damage equal to their weakness.
  • The wall imposes a –2 status penalty to Perception checks to sense creatures or objects on the other side.
name: Monsoon Curtain

At 3rd level and every 2 levels thereafter, increase the initial damage by 1d10 and the splash damage by 1.

<title level="1" right="Relic Major Gift">[Bottomless Reservoir](/Relics.aspx?ID=193)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Extradimensional" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=596" />
<trait label="Water" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=732" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 319

<row gap="medium">
**Aspect** Water

</row>

</column>

---

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))    **Effect** Your relic can absorb and release water, storing it in an endless reservoir. You either touch your relic to an adjacent body of water and absorb 1 gallon of it into the reservoir, or you release 1 gallon of a liquid from the reservoir. You can add another action to the activation to absorb water from a touched creature made entirely of water, like a [water elemental](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=45). If you do, it takes 1d6 damage per relic level ([basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=429) Fortitude save).

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))  **Trigger** You are targeted by an [acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=3) or [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=165) effect;  **Effect** Your relic attempts to counteract the effect, expending 1 gallon of stored liquid. At the GM's discretion, you can use this ability on effects made of other liquids, such as blood.
name: Bottomless Reservoir

<title level="1" right="Relic Grand Gift">[Ripples and Waves](/Relics.aspx?ID=195)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Water" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=732" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 319

<row gap="medium">
**Aspect** Water

</row>

</column>

---

You become attuned to the ebb and flow of all things, as if embodying water itself. You gain wavesense 60 feet as a [precise](/Rules.aspx?ID=411) sense and can cast 5th-rank [_hydraulic push_](/Spells.aspx?ID=154) at will as an innate spell.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** Ankle-deep water floods outward from you, filling a 60-foot emanation centered on you for 1 minute. Enemies within the area without a swim Speed treat the area as [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453) for movement on land. While the effect persists, you can have any [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=165) effects you generate originate from any point within the emanation, in addition to their normal range and area.
name: Ripples and Waves

**Source** [Pathfinder #200: Seven Dooms for Sandpoint](/Sources.aspx?ID=229) pg. 185 **Aspect** Celestial --- **Activate** command, Interact; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** _Monarch_ opens a conduit to Desna's palace of Cynosure and infuses your soul with the grace of the azatas who dance and frolic therein. You gain 50 additional Hit Points, increasing both your maximum and current Hit Points, as well as weakness 10 to lawful damage. You also gain the effects of the [righteous call](/Relics.aspx?ID=17) gift, without expending that gift's daily use.
You can Dismiss the effect. If you do so, you unleash a wave of chaotic power in the form of a rainbow-like pulse of light, causing the relic to cast [_scintillating pattern_](/Spells.aspx?ID=267). The effects of this casting of scintillating pattern don't affect your allies.
name: Azata's Grace
primary_source_raw: Pathfinder #200: Seven Dooms for Sandpoint pg. 185

<title level="1" right="Relic Minor Gift">[Feather Steps](/Relics.aspx?ID=2)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Air" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=5" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />

**Aspect** Air

</column>

---

While wearing the relic, you do not trigger traps that use weight or pressure plates as a trigger. Also, whenever you fall, you reduce the falling damage by the level of the relic. If this would reduce the falling damage to 0, you land on your feet and are not prone. The relic grants a +1 item bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) checks made to Jump, increasing to +2 at 9th level and +3 at 17th level.

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Relic Minor Gift">[Living Death](/Relics.aspx?ID=20)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Necromancy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=117" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Gamemastery Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=22) pg. 98

<row gap="medium">
**Aspect** Death

</row>

</column>

---

You stand on the threshold between the living and the dead. If you attempt a saving throw against an effect that would deal negative damage to you, such as [_harm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=146), your outcome is one degree of success better than what you rolled. If you roll a critical success and the effect is capable of healing undead, you regain HP equal to half the spell’s full damage. Any [positive](/Traits.aspx?ID=128) spell or effect that would heal you has only half the normal effect. All these effects apply only if you are a living creature.
name: Living Death

<traits>

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> command, Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You shroud yourself with powers from beyond the living realm for 1 minute. While this shroud is active, you take on a pallid appearance and gain poison resistance equal to half the relic’s level and a +1 status bonus to saving throws against [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [diseases](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), effects that would make you [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), and [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145) effects. In addition, the DC for your recovery checks is 9 + your dying value instead of 10 + your dying value.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You or a creature within 30 feet of you rolls two d20s for a [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=612) or [misfortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=654) effect and you like the higher roll;  **Effect** You steal the higher of the two numbers the triggering target rolled; the target must use the other result. Until you make your next [daily preparations](/Rules.aspx?ID=2575), you can substitute that number for one d20 roll made for a saving throw or skill check you have to make against an effect created by an enemy, hazard, or the environment. Doing so is a [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=612) effect. If you fail to do so before your next daily preparations, you lose the stored number.

<title level="1" right="Relic Major Gift">[Pierce the Veil](/Relics.aspx?ID=22)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Necromancy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=117" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Gamemastery Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=22) pg. 99

<row gap="medium">
**Aspect** Death

**Prerequisite** living death gift
</row>

</column>

---

The relic allows you to pierce the veil between life and death. While you are holding or wearing the relic, you have the negative healing ability (as though you were undead; positive energy harms you and negative energy heals you). In addition, whenever you are healed by a negative spell or effect, you gain resistance 5 to all damage (except force, [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=297), and positive) until the start of your next turn. Finally, you gain a +2 item bonus to all saves against [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects while you are the bearer of the relic; this increases to a +3 item bonus at 13th level.
name: Pierce the Veil

<title level="1" right="Relic Minor Gift">[Intelligent Relic](/Relics.aspx?ID=221)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Intelligent" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=280" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 200

<row gap="medium">
**Aspect** Intelligent Relic

</row>

</column>

---

Your relic becomes an intelligent item. It has an alignment within one step of yours on each axis if not matching yours. It gains an alignment trait matching its alignment. The relic possesses precise sight at a range of 30 feet and imprecise hearing at a range of 30 feet. It is trained in Perception, increasing its proficiency when you increase yours. Choose whether it can speak aloud or communicate with you telepathically. It knows languages fitting its backstory, usually from among those you know. The relic is also trained in three skills, increasing one skill’s proficiency each time the relic gains a new gift. Your relic’s mental ability scores begin at +3, +2, and +1, arranged as you choose. The relic is also trained in Will saves, and its proficiency increases as yours does. Each time you gain a new gift, increase one of the relic’s mental ability scores by 2 or two by 1. The relic otherwise functions as normal for an intelligent item.
name: Intelligent Relic

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Relic Grand Gift">[Grim Specter](/Relics.aspx?ID=24)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Necromancy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=117" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Gamemastery Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=22) pg. 99

<row gap="medium">
**Aspect** Death

</row>

</column>

---

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, envision;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You can call upon the terrifying powers that fuel your relic to transform yourself into a specter of death. You become incorporeal, having no physical body. You are immune to effects that require a physical body, including precision damage as well as exposure to most diseases and poisons. You also have resistance to all damage equal to half the relic’s level; force damage, [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=297) attacks, negative damage, and positive damage ignore this resistance, and the resistance doubles against non-magical damage.

 You can move through solid objects. When inside an object, you can’t perceive, attack, or Interact with anything outside it, and you’re always [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) on turns you start in an object. Corporeal creatures can pass through you but can’t end their movement in your space. You can’t attempt Strength-based checks against corporeal creatures or objects, unless those objects have the _ghost touch_ property rune. Likewise, corporeal creatures can’t attempt Strength-based checks against you.

 You can Sustain the Activation up to 1 minute.
name: Grim Specter

<title level="1" right="Relic Minor Gift">[Ease Burden](/Relics.aspx?ID=25)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Earth" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=55" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Gamemastery Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=22) pg. 99

<row gap="medium">
**Aspect** Earth

</row>

</column>

---

Your relic draws strength from the earth underfoot to lighten your load. As long as your relic is on your person and you are standing on the ground, you can carry 1 more Bulk than normal before becoming encumbered, and you increase your maximum Bulk by 2. At 6th level and every 4 levels thereafter, increase the Bulk you can carry before becoming encumbered by 1 and your maximum Bulk by 2.
name: Ease Burden

<title level="1" right="Relic Minor Gift">[Rolling Geode](/Relics.aspx?ID=26)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Earth" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=55" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Gamemastery Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=22) pg. 99

<row gap="medium">
**Aspect** Earth

</row>

</column>

---

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, envision;  **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect**  Stone and crystal form a sphere that chases your foes. You create a geode in an unoccupied square within 30 feet. You can spend a single action, which has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) trait, to move the geode up to 30 feet along the ground. If the geode enters a creature’s space, the creature must succeed at a basic Reflex save or take 2d6 bludgeoning damage; a creature can take damage this way only once per turn, even if you roll the geode through its space multiple times. The geode persists for up to 1 minute or until destroyed or Dismissed; it has AC 5, Hardness 10, and 40 Hit Points, and is immune to critical hits and precision damage.
name: Rolling Geode

---

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, Interact;  **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** Seismic pulses from your relic ripple through the nearby ground, shaking sharp fragments to the surface. A 10-foot-radius patch of stone or earth within 60 feet is transformed into difficult terrain. A creature that enters an affected square during a [move](/Traits.aspx?ID=114) action must succeed at an Acrobatics check or Reflex save or take 1d6 piercing damage; it needs to roll only once per move action even if it moves through several squares. At 6th level and every 4 levels thereafter, increase the damage by 1d6.

<title level="1" right="Relic Major Gift">[Underground Bounty](/Relics.aspx?ID=29)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Earth" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=55" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Gamemastery Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=22) pg. 99

<row gap="medium">
**Aspect** Earth

</row>

</column>

---

Your relic produces metals and stones to empower your weapons. Each day during your daily preparations, your relic conjures a magical shard of [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=379), [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375), gold, or a non-precious stone or metal material of your choice (such as bronze or granite). If your relic is a weapon, it immediately absorbs the shard, causing it to gain a magical sheen of that metal or stone until your next daily preparations. If your relic is not a weapon, the shard is not immediately used, and you can touch it to a stone or metal weapon using an Interact action to give it a magical sheen of that metal or stone for 10 minutes. While coated in the magical sheen, the weapon interacts with weaknesses, resistances, and the like as if it were made of the stone or metal from the shard rather than its own. The sheen and the shard are obviously magical in nature, and if you don’t use the shard, it disappears when you make your next daily preparations. At 17th level, add [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=374) and [mithral](/Equipment.aspx?ID=377) to the available options.
name: Underground Bounty

<title level="1" right="Relic Minor Gift">[Little Helper](/Relics.aspx?ID=33)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Evil" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=64" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Gamemastery Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=22) pg. 100

<row gap="medium">
**Aspect** Fiend

</row>

</column>

---

Your relic awakens to sapience, developing a personality (usually one that clashes with your own). Your relic has an alignment corresponding to the type of fiend associated with the item, precise vision and imprecise hearing each out to 30 feet, the ability to understand and speak one language you know, and Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma modifiers of +0. Your item’s total Perception and Will modifiers are equal to your proficiency modifiers in Perception and Will, respectively. It is trained in the [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) associated with its fiend type (such as Demon Lore), for a total modifier of 2 + the relic’s level. Unlike most intelligent items, the relic has no actions or reactions and can’t use any of its own activations.
name: Little Helper

<traits>
<trait label="Evil" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=64" />

</row>

---

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** Profane energies twist your weapons, filling them with rage toward everything good. For 1 minute, your weapons and unarmed attacks gain the [_unholy_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=307) weapon rune while you hold them. If a weapon is at its limit on property runes, you can choose one property rune on the weapon to go dormant while the _unholy_ rune takes effect. In addition, successful Strikes your allies make while they’re within 30 feet of you deal 1 additional evil damage.

<title level="1" right="Relic Grand Gift">[Command Legion](/Relics.aspx?ID=36)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Gamemastery Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=22) pg. 100

<row gap="medium">
**Aspect** Fiend

</row>

</column>

---

**Activate** <actions string="Three Actions" /> command, envision;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** Your relic opens a gate within itself, overwhelming your enemies with fiends. You cast 6th-level [_summon fiend_](/Spells.aspx?ID=323), but summon two fiends instead of one, and you can command them both with 1 action to Sustain a Spell. If the relic has the profane fervor gift, this activation grants the effects of that gift as well, without expending its daily use. For the next minute, you can use the following activation.

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> envision;   **Trigger** One of the fiends summoned by command legion is banished;  **Effect** You summon that same fiend again in an adjacent space. The fiend returns at full Hit Points and all conditions, spells, and other effects on it end before you summon it again. However, it does not recover any limited-use abilities it used before, such as innate spells or abilities with a Frequency entry.
name: Command Legion

<title level="1" right="Relic Minor Gift">[Heat Haze](/Relics.aspx?ID=38)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Abjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=2" />
<trait label="Fire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=72" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Gamemastery Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=22) pg. 101

<row gap="medium">
**Aspect** Fire

</row>

</column>

---

Your relic’s heat keeps the air around you pleasantly warm. While you are holding or wearing the relic, it protects you from [severe environmental cold](/Rules.aspx?ID=642). At 9th level, it also protects you from extreme cold, and at 17th level, it protects you from incredible cold. At 4th level, the relic also gains the following activation.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, envision;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** Your relic erratically elevates the air temperature around you, creating heat shimmers that distort your appearance and grant you the [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) condition for 1 minute. As the nature of this effect still leaves your location obvious, you can’t use this concealment to [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=62) or [Sneak](/Actions.aspx?ID=63).
name: Heat Haze

Your heatsight can see temperature gradients out to a range of 30 feet, allowing you to detect living creatures and warm objects even in the dark. <title level="1" right="Relic Grand Gift">[Blazing Soul](/Relics.aspx?ID=42)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Fire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=72" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Gamemastery Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=22) pg. 101

<row gap="medium">
**Aspect** Fire

</row>

</column>

---

Fire magic suffuses your body and soul, protecting you from lesser flames and allowing you to kindle even the smallest sparks into powerful infernos. You gain fire resistance 10. The first time each hour you are targeted by a [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) effect that would deal damage, you regain 1d8 HP for every counteract level of the effect, in addition to taking the damage. 

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> envision;   **Trigger** A creature within 60 feet is critically hit by or critically fails a save against a fire effect and is not reduced to 0 Hit Points;  **Effect** Flames roar forth from the triggering creature, dealing 6d6 fire damage to all creatures in a 15-foot burst centered on it, including that creature (basic Reflex). You aren’t affected by the activation, though your allies are. If this damage reduces the triggering creature to 0 Hit Points, it is reduced to a fine ash, though its gear remains. You can’t use this activation again for 1d4 rounds.
name: Blazing Soul

You gain an item bonus equal to half the relic’s level (minimum 1) to the Hit Points you recover from positive healing spells; this bonus applies only the first time per casting that a particular spell heals you.

<traits>
<trait label="Healing" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=89" />
<trait label="Necromancy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=117" />

---

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> envision;   **Trigger** An ally within 60 feet regains more Hit Points than it is currently down via a targeted positive healing effect without a duration;  **Effect** Your relic acts as a relay for vitality. You or an adjacent willing creature regain a number of Hit Points equal to the amount in excess of the triggering creature’s maximum Hit Points; for instance, if the triggering creature was missing 5 Hit Points and was targeted by a [_heal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=148) spell that restored 12 Hit Points, you or an adjacent willing creature would regain the remaining 7 Hit Points.
name: Vitality Siphon

<traits>
<trait label="Healing" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=89" />
<trait label="Necromancy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=117" />

</row>

---

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** Massive amounts of positive energy surge forth. For 1 minute, you emit positive energy in a 10-foot emanation centered on you. At the end of your turn, you (if you are living) and allied living creatures in the emanation regain 10 Hit Points. If you or an allied creature would die due to an increased [dying](/Conditions.aspx?ID=11) value, you can Dismiss the activation as a free action to prevent the death; if you do, their dying value doesn’t increase.
name: Life Everlasting

Your relic is imbued with the psychic impressions of ages past. While the relic is on your person, you are trained in 3 additional [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) skills of the GM’s choice that fit the relic’s history, decided at the time of gaining this gift. If the relic is 9th level, you instead have expert proficiency in these Lore skills, and if the relic is 17th level, you have master proficiency in these Lore skills.

**Source** [Gamemastery Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=22) pg. 102 **Aspect** Mind --- **Activate** envision; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** Your relic reaches into the minds of nearby creatures and blocks your presence from their perceptions. For the next 10 minutes, a creature that enters a 120-foot-radius emanation, centered on you, must attempt a secret Will save, after which the creature is then temporarily immune for 1 day. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes a –2 status penalty to Perception checks to [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=84) or otherwise detect you.
**Failure** You filter all of the creature’s senses, making it difficult for it to notice you. When you are [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=22) or [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=39) from the creature, it doesn’t observe you as soon as you do anything other than [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=62), [Sneak](/Actions.aspx?ID=63), or Step. If it rolls a Perception check to Seek or otherwise detect you, it gets the outcome one degree of success worse than what it rolled, and if you roll a [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) check to Hide, Sneak, or otherwise escape its attention, you get the outcome one degree of success better than what you rolled against the creature. If you use a hostile action toward the creature, the effect ends for that creature after your hostile action is completed.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the effect doesn’t end for that creature if you use a hostile action against it.
name: Perception Filter

Your relic grows potent natural medicines. Each night, two medicinal herbs (or other plants) grow from your relic and can be harmlessly harvested during your next daily preparations. A living creature that consumes an herb with an Interact action regains 1d8 Hit Points for every 2 relic levels (minimum 1d8). At 9th level, the relic grows three herbs each night, and at 17th level, the relic grows four herbs each night. Herbs wither away 1 day after being harvested.

Make an attack roll as you would when using a [grappling hook,](/Equipment.aspx?ID=23) but if you roll a critical failure on the check to secure the vine, you get a failure instead. Once the vine is anchored, creatures receive a +1 status bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) checks to [Climb](/Actions.aspx?ID=33) the vine, and they gain a +5-foot status bonus to the distance they move with a successful check while Climbing using the vine.

 The vine lasts for 1 day or until you use Grappling Vine again, at which point your older vine withers.

<title level="1" right="Relic Major Gift">[Petal Dance](/Relics.aspx?ID=59)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Plant" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=125" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

**Aspect** Plant

</row>

</column>

---

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** You discorporate into a cloud of petals and leaves. This grants you the [swarm](/Traits.aspx?ID=239) trait, immunity to falling damage, resistance 5 to bludgeoning and slashing damage, and weakness 5 to area and splash damage. You can fit into spaces only a few inches wide, moving your constituent petals through the gap. You don’t gain the swarm mind ability, so you are still affected normally by [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106) effects. As a swarm, you can’t speak, cast spells, use [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104) actions requiring your hands, activate your magic items, or make any of your Strikes with your normal body. You remain in this form for 1 minute or until you Dismiss the activation. At 13th level, the relic gains a reaction that triggers when you fall or take damage, applying this gift’s resistances, weaknesses, and immunity to falling damage to the triggering effect.

markdown:
**Source** [Gamemastery Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=22) pg. 104 **Aspect** Plant --- **Activate** command, Interact; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** With a pulse of natural energy, your relic grows massive plants in an instant. The megaflora is of one of the following types, chosen by the GM when the relic gains this gift. A megaflora appears in an unoccupied 10-foot space within 30 feet and has 50 Hit Points, weakness to fire 5, 37 AC, +20 Reflex, and +30 Fortitude. It persists for 1 minute or until reduced to 0 Hit Points.
  • **Corpseflower** A single putrid-smelling flower grows at the target location. While the flower persists, each round at the end of your turn, all creatures in a 20-foot burst centered on the plant except you must succeed at a Fortitude save or be [sickened 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) (also stunned 2 on a critical failure). This is a [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126) effect.
  • **Thorns** A 10-foot-tall thorned plant or bamboo stalk grows at the target location. While the stalk persists, each round at the end of your turn, sharp stakes erupt from the ground, dealing 6d8 piercing damage to all creatures in a 20-foot burst centered on the plant except you (basic Reflex)
  • **Tree of Life** A large tree bearing life-giving fruit grows at the target location. While the tree persists, living creatures within reach of the tree can use an Interact action to pick and eat one of the tree’s fruits, regaining 2d8+5 Hit Points; picking a fruit without eating it causes the fruit to instantly vanish. This is a [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=89) effect, and a given creature can heal from the tree only once per round.
name: Megaflora

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Relic Minor Gift">[Encompassing Darkness](/Relics.aspx?ID=61)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Shadow" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=143" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Gamemastery Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=22) pg. 104

<row gap="medium">
**Aspect** Shadow

</row>

</column>

---

The shadows at your feet can hold objects. You can Interact with your shadow to store or remove objects, just like you would a mundane container. Your shadow can contain 3 Bulk of objects, which don’t count toward the Bulk you are carrying. At 6th level, and every 4 levels thereafter, you can store 1 additional Bulk of objects in your shadow. While the items are in your shadow and can be detected normally, you gain a +4 circumstance bonus to [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) checks to [Conceal the Objects](/Actions.aspx?ID=61) unless someone knows to check your shadow for items.
name: Encompassing Darkness

<traits>

<trait label="Shadow" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=143" />

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Gamemastery Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=22) pg. 104

<row gap="medium">
**Aspect** Shadow

</row>

</column>

---

Your relic absorbs light and wraps shadow around you, hiding you in darkness. As long as you are in dim light in an area of shadows, you can attempt a [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) check to [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=62), even if you aren’t concealed against the creature, such as with a creature with [darkvision](/Rules.aspx?ID=415).
name: Obscure

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Relic Major Gift">[Dancing Shadow](/Relics.aspx?ID=64)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Shadow" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=143" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Gamemastery Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=22) pg. 104

<row gap="medium">
**Aspect** Shadow

**Prerequisite** The relic is a weapon.
</row>

</column>

---

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, Interact;  **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** Your relic’s shadow detaches from your relic and dances through the air to attack an enemy. When you Activate the relic, designate a target. The relic’s shadow flies up to 60 feet until it is adjacent to that foe and then makes a Strike against it, dealing 5d8 damage on a success or double that on a critical success; the damage type is of any type normally dealt by your relic. The shadow uses your attack bonus with the relic, and it uses and contributes to your multiple attack penalty. While this activation is in effect, you can use a single action, which has the [attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=15) and [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) traits, to mentally direct the shadow to make another Strike against the same target. The shadow lasts until that target is reduced to 0 Hit Points, that target moves more than 400 feet from you, or that target moves to an area where no shadow could be cast, such as an area of complete darkness, whichever comes first. 

The shadow doesn’t take up space, grant flanking, or have any other attributes a creature would, and it automatically follows the chosen foe. The shadow can’t make any attack other than its Strike, and feats or spells that affect weapons do not apply to it.
name: Dancing Shadow

<title level="1" right="Relic Major Gift">[Dark Roads](/Relics.aspx?ID=65)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Shadow" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=143" />
<trait label="Teleportation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=156" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Gamemastery Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=22) pg. 104

<row gap="medium">
**Aspect** Shadow

</row>

</column>

---

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, envision;     **Effect** Your relic creates a path from your shadow to a nearby one, teleporting you and any items you’re wearing or holding from your current space to an unoccupied one within 30 feet that you can see. You can’t use the dark roads gift again for 1d4 rounds. If the destination is not within an area of [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=407), the range is instead 60 feet. If this would bring another creature with you—even if you’re carrying it in an [extradimensional](/Traits.aspx?ID=67) container—the gift fails.
name: Dark Roads

---

Water collects at the tip of your relic. You can adjust your grip on your relic with an Interact action to allow you to make lashing current weapon Strikes with your relic, which deal 1d4 bludgeoning damage; have the [disarm](/Traits.aspx?ID=175), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [reach 10](/Traits.aspx?ID=192), [trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=196), and [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=200) traits; and are in the flail group. Your lashing current Strikes gain the benefit of your relic’s fundamental and property runes, though any property runes that would not be applicable to the lashing currents are not applied. You can use another Interact action to return to your normal grip and make Strikes with the relic.
name: Lashing Currents

<title level="1" right="Relic Major Gift">[Bottomless Reservoir](/Relics.aspx?ID=70)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Extradimensional" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=67" />
<trait label="Water" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=165" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Gamemastery Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=22) pg. 105

<row gap="medium">
**Aspect** Water

</row>

</column>

---

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact;     **Effect** Your relic can absorb and release water, storing it in a endless reservoir. You either touch your relic to an adjacent body of water and absorb 1 gallon of it into the reservoir, or you release 1 gallon of a liquid from the reservoir. You can add another action to the activation to absorb water from a touched creature made entirely of water, like a [water elemental](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=45). If you do, it takes 1d6 damage per relic level (basic Fortitude save).

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> Interact;  **Frequency** You are targeted by an [acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=3) or [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=165) effect;   **Effect** Your relic attempts to counteract the effect, expending 1 gallon of stored liquid. At the GM’s discretion, you can use this ability on effects made of other liquids, such as blood.
name: Bottomless Reservoir

<title level="1" right="Relic Grand Gift">[Ripples and Waves](/Relics.aspx?ID=72)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Water" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=165" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Gamemastery Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=22) pg. 105

<row gap="medium">
**Aspect** Water

</row>

</column>

---

You become attuned to the ebb and flow of all things. You gain wavesense 60 as a precise sense and can cast 5th-level [_hydraulic push_](/Spells.aspx?ID=154) at will as an innate spell.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, envision;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** Ankle-deep water floods outward from you, filling a 60-foot emanation centered on you for 1 minute. Enemies within the area without a swim Speed treat the area as difficult terrain for movement on land. While the effect persists, you can have any [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=165) effects you generate originate from any point within the emanation, in addition to their normal range and area.
name: Ripples and Waves

<title level="1" right="Relic Major Gift">[Draconic Arrogance](/Relics.aspx?ID=76)</title>

<traits>

**Aspect** Dragon

</column>

---

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> command;  **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You're about to roll a saving throw;  **Effect** You laugh at fate and revel in your draconic arrogance. If you roll a critical success on the saving throw, you can attempt to [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=53) the creature that created the effect that forced you to roll the saving throw (if any). If you roll a success, you critically succeed instead. If you roll a failure (but not a critical failure), you succeed instead. If you roll a critical failure, however, you're forced to face your own hubris and become [frightened 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19).
name: Draconic Arrogance

<title level="1" right="Relic Minor Gift">[Muse](/Relics.aspx?ID=87)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 198

<row gap="medium">
**Aspect** Artistry

**Prerequisite** The relic is 3rd level or higher.
</row>

</column>

---

The relic guides you in your art like a muse, giving you inspirational ideas and providing an item bonus to [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) or [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) checks. (Choose one. If your relic allows you to do so, you can take this gift again to choose the other.) If the relic is 3rd level or higher, you gain a +1 item bonus. Once the relic attains 9th level, the bonus increases to +2. The bonus increases to +3 when the relic is 17th level.
name: Muse

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))    **Effect** You channel your relic's emotion at a target within 30 feet, overpowering an [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60) effect on that creature. <title level="1" right="Relic Grand Gift">[Quickening Emotion](/Relics.aspx?ID=96)</title>

<traits>

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561))  **Trigger** You are the target of an [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60) effect from a hostile source;  **Effect** The relic attempts a [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) check. If the check succeeds, the relic absorbs the effect completely, negating it. Instead, you're [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=32) for up to 1 minute, but no longer than the triggering effect's duration. You then become temporarily immune to this effect for 1 hour.
